title: Damage delivery
principles: Damage delivery describes how a valid damage instance reaches a target and must remain separate from attack rhythm.
Direct delivery should not be called hitscan unless the supplied data explicitly distinguishes hitscan from another direct mechanism.
Projectile-direct delivery may introduce travel behavior, but projectile speed and trajectory must not be invented when absent.
Projectile-radial delivery combines a projectile event with a spatial damage component; direct impact and radial damage may use different statistics.
Charged projectile components must be kept separate from uncharged projectile components.
Radial delivery provides spatial reach but does not automatically establish area control, reliable group clear, or equal damage across the radius.
Beam delivery should defer to the dedicated beam concept for maintained-contact behavior.
When several component types exist in one mode, each component must preserve its own evidence and restrictions.

direct: The supplied component reaches its target without explicit projectile or radial evidence.
projectile: The supplied component travels as a projectile.
Travel limitations remain undetermined without projectile-speed or trajectory evidence.
radial: The supplied component affects a spatial region.
Coverage and retained damage require radius, falloff, or equivalent evidence when available.
projectile_radial: The mode includes projectile travel followed by radial delivery.
Impact and radial components must not be merged into one unsupported statistic.
charged_projectile: The supplied charged component is distinct from the uncharged delivery.
Its statistics and operational cost must be evaluated separately.
beam: The supplied delivery uses maintained beam contact.
mixed: The mode contains multiple delivery components.
Each component must remain traceable to its supplied data.
undetermined: The supplied component data do not establish a reliable delivery type.

exceptions: Do not infer projectile speed from the projectile tag.
Do not infer explosion radius from a radial component alone.
Do not assume direct and radial components share critical or status behavior.
Do not call all direct components hitscan.
Do not treat every aoe compatibility tag as proof of a radial component.
